Header file for JavaScript execution functions on ESP8266, including entry, eval, loop, and exit.

# Purpose
This C header file defines the interface for a JavaScript engine's execution functions. It declares four functions: [`js_entry`](<#js_entry>), [`js_eval`](<#js_eval>), [`js_loop`](<#js_loop>), and [`js_exit`](<#js_exit>). The [`js_entry`](<#js_entry>) function initializes the execution environment with a JavaScript source code provided as a string and its size. The [`js_eval`](<#js_eval>) function evaluates the given JavaScript source code. The [`js_loop`](<#js_loop>) function likely manages the execution loop, taking a `ticknow` parameter to control timing or iteration. The [`js_exit`](<#js_exit>) function is used to terminate the execution environment. The header file includes guards to prevent multiple inclusions and supports C++ linkage with `extern "C"`.
